title: dryopteris elwesii
intro: Dryopteris elwesii, also known as Elwes' Male Fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is native to China and is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiparasitic and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris elwesii include its ability to treat tapeworm infestations, as it contains anthelmintic compounds that help to expel parasites from the body, and also has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, with herbal preparations often being made from the rhizome and leaves in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions.
